characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A failed actor and perpetual underachiever who rejoins Party Down catering at the beginning of the show. He is unable to get away from his catchphrase from a beer commercial, "Are we having fun yet?" Catchphrase: Henry was in a beer commercial where he said, "Are we having fun yet?" He hates it when people recognize him and ask him to say it. The Cynic: Unlike the other members of the catering team, Henry has given up all hope of ever being a successful actor. Embarrassing Ad Gig: Henry became famous for his role in a beer commercial that spawned the in-universe catchphrase "Are we having fun yet?!". He thinks the role killed his chances of having a real acting career, and he dreads being recognized in public. Four-Temperament Ensemble: Melancholic.

characters.name: Henry Pollard
characters.description: A failed actor and perpetual underachiever who rejoins Party Down catering at the beginning of the show. He is unable to get away from his catchphrase from a beer commercial, "Are we having fun yet?" Catchphrase: Henry was in a beer commercial where he said, "Are we having fun yet?" He hates it when people recognize him and ask him to say it. The Cynic: Unlike the other members of the catering team, Henry has given up all hope of ever being a successful actor. Embarrassing Ad Gig: Henry became famous for his role in a beer commercial that spawned the in-universe catchphrase "Are we having fun yet?!". He thinks the role killed his chances of having a real acting career, and he dreads being recognized in public. Four-Temperament Ensemble: Melancholic.

characters.name: Ron Donald
characters.description: Ron is Party Down's bumbling team leader who takes great pride in his job. His dream is to manage a Soup R' Crackers, a franchise that offers all-you-can-eat soup. Berserk Button: Ron goes off when the group is in danger of getting poor client feedback cards or when his reputation is at stake. Examples of this include threatening to destroy the teen girl's life in "Willow Canyon Homeowners Annual Party" and desecrating the American Flag in "California College Conservative Union Caucus." Butt-Monkey: Ron is a pedantic, sycophantic, socially inept loser who thinks he may well be the next Donald Trump. He's also put through more mental and physical trauma than any other character on the show. The Determinator: Say what you like about Ron but nobody can question his drive or passion to achieve. Glory Days: As shown in the class reunion episode, Ron has a complicated relation to his past.

characters.name: Casey Klein
characters.description: Casey is struggling to make it as a comedic actress and resents authority, particularly Ron. Awful Wedded Life: Casey and her husband clash often, and get divorced during Season 1. Four-Temperament Ensemble: Phlegmatic. Informed Judaism: Casey's religion is never brought up in Season 1. In Season 2, she makes a couple references to being Jewish, though it doesn't factor into her actions. Actress Lizzy Caplan is Jewish in real life. Only Sane Employee: Along with Henry, she's a rare level-headed caterer at Party Down.

characters.name: Kyle Bradway
characters.description: Kyle is a wannabe actor/musician/model, and not the brightest tool in the shed. Brainless Beauty: Kyle is good-looking and not particularly intelligent. Bromantic Foil: To Roman and his more pretentious outlook on life. Fratbro: He's certainly the most likely to start the party. Four-Temperament Ensemble: Sanguine.

characters.name: Roman De Beers
characters.description: A pretentious and judgmental screenwriter, who primarily writes hard science fiction. Berserk Button: Roman can't stand it when people mix up his precious science fiction with hated fantasy. He even ruins a sure thing with a porn star when she mentions that she likes dragons. Four-Temperament Ensemble: Choleric. Jerkass: Roman is unpleasant and rude to most of the people he interacts with. Kyle is a frequent target. He even ruins a conversation with a porn star right when it turned out they had some connection and could actually hit it off when she reveals that the literature she reads is fantasy, which, given his obsession with accuracy in all forms of media, leads him to blow up and end it prematurely. Lust Object: He sees Casey as this. He even goes so far as to insist there is a line.

characters.name: Constance Carmell
characters.description: A former B-movie actress with a cheerful and optimistic disposition. Age-Gap Romance: Constance and her fiance Howard in "Constance Carmell Wedding". Howard's daughter shows up halfway into the party and turns out to be Constance's age. The Ditz: Constance is quirky and often distracted from whatever her job calls for her to be doing. Meaningful Name: Constance is named for the fact that in her pursuit of her dreams and her optimism, she's completely constant. No Sense of Personal Space:

characters.name: Bobbie St. Brown
characters.description: Constance's roommate who replaces her on the Party Down team after her absence. Convenient Replacement Character: Bobbie shows up the episode after Constance leaves. Justified, as they were roommates and Constance could easily give her the job. Pollyana: She wants her coworkers to smile and listen to dolphin noises

characters.name: Lydia Dunfree
characters.description: A stage mom and recent divorcée who joins Party Down in the second season, seeking to make her daughter a star. The Ditz: Lydia is naïve and will take advice from anyone about Hollywood. Stage Mom: Wants her 13-year-old daughter, Escapade, to become a big Hollywood star. Suspiciously Similar Substitute: Lydia replaces Constance in Season 2. They're both weird, middle-aged, female ditzes. Lydia eventually gets more fleshed out and she moves away from being too similar to Constance, but the fact that she replaced her is lampshaded in "Constance Carmell Wedding." Cloudcuckoolander: A bit scattered and all over the place.
